name: recvcap
status: CANDIDATE / NOT in DEFAULT_STACK -- opt-in only. ROOT-CAUSE MODEL REVISED 2026-06-16 after live FUN_00404610 al=1 confirmation. No same-length byte patch is yet justified; C1/C2 are now PROVEN no-ops for the 0x2006 drop. The actionable next step is a function-boundary live probe (spec below) to read the runtime per-opcode handler slot, NOT a byte patch. Do not apply any patch from this file until the probe pins the divergence.

revision_2026_06_16.supersedes: The prior model (recv-pump factory FUN_00612510 returns NULL for 0x2006) is REFUTED by live evidence: factory returns a NON-NULL _mpsClientMessage32 object (vtable 0x66c0d8) for 0x2006, FUN_00404610 returns al=1, and extractor/decipher/recv all succeed. The frame IS received, decrypted, framed, and a message object IS created. The drop is strictly in the post-parse handler-dispatch routing, before classify/enqueue.
fully_reconstructed_receive_chain: pump @0x6122c0: per extracted frame -> FUN_00612510(opcode) creates message object (factory, [conn+0x10].vtable+8). LIVE: non-NULL, vtable=0x66c0d8 (_mpsClientMessage32).

FUN_00404210: calls cVar1=FUN_00404610(this+0x14, this+6=hdr, msghdrbuf). IF al!=0 -> RETURN IMMEDIATELY (no body read, no FUN_00610420). IF al==0 -> size-gate (C2 @0x404254) + FUN_00610420 body read.

FUN_00404610 (the inline dispatcher): param_1=msgobj, param_1[1]=[this+4]=lobby parse-system. *param_3 (hdr word) ==0x8000? -> system branch ([parsesys.vtbl+0x18]). ELSE: cVar3=[parsesys.vtbl+0x14](this+3, opcode) = handler LOOKUP. If cVar3==0 -> *(this+0xc)=0; RETURN al=0 (handler-not-found). If cVar3!=0 (handler FOUND, stored at *(this+0xc)): inline-dispatch (*[*(this+0xc)])(hdr,buf) + [*(this+0xc)+8](hdr, [parsesys+0x18], [parsesys+0x1c]); RETURN al=1.
=> al=1 means a per-opcode handler WAS found AND inline-dispatched. LIVE confirms al=1 for BOTH 0x2004 and 0x2006, so a handler is registered for 0x2006 too.

the found handler's vtbl[0]/vtbl[8] perform the actual delivery. The single client path to enqueue is FUN_004ae0d0 (vtbl[0] of the conn message-receive sub-object @ [conn+0x18], vtable 0x66e0f0). FUN_004ae0d0(this,opcode,_,buf): for opcode&0xffff != 0x204 -> FUN_004b8850(opcode,buf) ENQUEUE. It treats 0x2004 and 0x2006 IDENTICALLY.

enqueue FUN_004b8850: calls classifier FUN_004b8b00(opcode) -> size (0x2004=0x6dc, 0x2006=0x5304, BOTH valid, opcode-agnostic) -> malloc+copy into 500-slot queue @ base+0x3552b8 (stride 0x14), stores opcode @ +0x3552bc.
queue-pump FUN_004b8950: picks ready slot, calls dispatcher FUN_004ba2b0(queuedOpcode, queuedPayload).

dispatcher FUN_004ba2b0: TOP one-shot guard `if (byte[base+0x3579cd]) { clear; return; }`. switchVar local_3c = param_2 & 0xffff. case 0x2004 -> copy 0x1b7 dwords to base+0x35975c. case 0x2006 -> copy 0x14c1 dwords to base+0x359e3c. STRUCTURALLY IDENTICAL cases.

read-side FUN_00593cf0(slab, idx): phase1 sends FUN_004b78a0(1,7,&idx) = request 0x2005 (expects 0x2006); phase2 copies base+0x359e3c into slab. FSM FUN_0051a370 state 0x13 -> FUN_00593cf0(2); state 0x2d -> FUN_00593cf0(0)+render picker FUN_005946d0.
revision_2026_06_16.where_0x2004_and_0x2006_provably_diverge: Everything from FUN_004ae0d0 onward (enqueue/classify/queue/dispatcher) is opcode-agnostic between 0x2004 and 0x2006. The ONLY opcode-specific branch on this path is the per-opcode HANDLER object resolved by FUN_00404610's lookup ([parsesys.vtbl+0x14] -> per-opcode handler at a sorted registry, found via FUN_00405250 binary search; slots also reachable via FUN_00446a50/FUN_00446ab0/FUN_00446b10 which index opcode-0x2000 into arrays at [parsesys+4+idx*4] and [parsesys+0x34+idx*4]). The 0x2004 handler's vtbl[0] reaches FUN_004ae0d0->enqueue (proven by live enqueue of 0x2004). The 0x2006 handler is registered (al=1) but its inline dispatch does NOT reach enqueue (proven by live: classifier+dispatcher never see 0x2006). CONCLUSION: the registered handler OBJECT for opcode 0x2006 differs from 0x2004's, OR a shared handler applies an opcode/sequence gate inside its own vtbl[0]/vtbl[8]. WHICH concrete handler sits in the 0x2006 slot is RUNTIME-CONSTRUCTED and cannot be resolved by static RE (the registry array [parsesys+4/0x34+idx*4] is populated at runtime via FUN_00404800 regist_parse / FUN_004034f0 vector-insert).
revision_2026_06_16.secondary_silent_drop_gate_found: FUN_004029f0 (mpsCTLobbyMsgParseSystemImp::parse_message, vtbl slot @0x66bf44) is a SEPARATE delivery variant that, when a handler is found, runs TWO silent gates before dispatch: gate1 @0x402ae3 = [(msgobj+0x54).vtbl+0x10] = FUN_004045a0 = `return byte[(msgobj+0x54)+0x14]`; gate2 @0x402b02 = [(msgobj+0x54).vtbl+0xc] = FUN_00404580 = `return ([(+0x54)+8]!=0 || [(+0x54)+0xc]!=0)`. Either gate returning 0 => silent return (NO dispatch, NO throw). These flags are set only AFTER the body is read (FUN_00610420). NOTE: FUN_00404610's al=1 path returns BEFORE the body read in FUN_00404210, so if delivery actually went through FUN_004029f0 (rather than FUN_00404610's own inline dispatch) the gates would be 0. But live shows 0x2004 DOES deliver, so the live lobby path uses FUN_00404610's inline dispatch, not FUN_004029f0's gated dispatch. FUN_004029f0 is recorded as the alternative variant to probe if the handler-slot probe is inconclusive.

verified_static_facts.msgobj_vtable_0x66c0d8: vtbl[0]=FUN_00404180 ctor; vtbl[2](+8)=FUN_00404210 parse; vtbl[5](+0x14)=FUN_00402d70. ctor FUN_004041a0 sets *this=0x66c0d8 and this[0x15](=+0x54)=0x66c0f0.
verified_static_facts.subobj_vtable_0x66c0f0: vtbl[3](+0xc)=FUN_00404580, vtbl[4](+0x10)=FUN_004045a0 -- the two FUN_004029f0 gates.
verified_static_facts.parser_vtable_0x66cd20: vtbl[5](+0x14)=FUN_004469f0 (ret 0x5304 maxlen), vtbl[6](+0x18)=FUN_00446a00 (ret 0x2000 opbase), vtbl[8](+0x20)=FUN_00446be0 (range-dispatch over idx 0..0xb), vtbl[9](+0x24)=FUN_00446a50 (lookup [parsesys+4+idx*4]), vtbl[10](+0x28)=FUN_00446b10 (name-lookup), vtbl[11](+0x2c)=FUN_00446ab0 (lookup [parsesys+0x34+idx*4]).
verified_static_facts.opcode_name_table_0x765cb8: 12 entries idx0..0xb = 0x2000..0x200b. idx4=0x2004 LobbyResponseInformationCharacterCharge; idx6=0x2006 LobbyResponseInformationSession.
verified_static_facts.enqueue_handler_vtable_0x66e0f0: vtbl[0]=FUN_004ae0d0 (the ONLY caller of enqueue FUN_004b8850). Installed by conn ctors FUN_004ad780/FUN_004adac0 as [conn+0x18].
verified_static_facts.dispatcher_FUN_004ba2b0: switchVar = param_2 & 0xffff (the queued opcode). case 0x2004 -> base+0x35975c (0x1b7 dwords); case 0x2006 -> base+0x359e3c (0x14c1 dwords). Identical structure. Top guard byte[base+0x3579cd] one-shot swallow.
verified_static_facts.request_sender_FUN_004b78a0: case 6 (param=7) sends 0x2005 expects 0x2006 (NO precondition flag); case 5 (param=6) sends 0x2003 expects 0x2004. FUN_00593cf0 phase1 uses case 6 (0x2005). FUN_0051bde0 uses FUN_004b78a0(1,5,..) = 0x2003 (the 0x2004 flow).
verified_static_facts.byte_stability: C1@0x2e7f=7717, C2@0x4254=7717, pump-je@0x21234c=742a are byte-identical across _pristine.exe, G7MTClient.korean.exe, G7MTClient.playable.exe, and G7MTClient.korean.menufix.exe (verified 2026-06-16 by direct file read). Drift-check will pass for any of these.

C1_C2_reevaluation: C1 (NOP ja @0x402e7f in FUN_00402e30) and C2 (NOP ja @0x404254 in FUN_00404210) sit in the `cVar1==0` (FUN_00404610 returned al=0) branch -- the body-read path. LIVE confirms FUN_00404610 returns al=1 for 0x2006, so this branch is NEVER ENTERED for the failing opcode. Therefore C1/C2 are DEFINITIVELY no-ops for the 0x2006 picker drop (upgraded from 'likely no-op' to 'proven no-op'). They remain recorded ONLY as drift-verified bytes for documentation; they MUST NOT be applied as a fix for this bug.
pump_je_0x61234c_reevaluation: Still REJECTED. It is the factory-NULL teardown `je 0x612378`. Live confirms the factory returns NON-NULL for 0x2006, so this je is NOT taken for 0x2006 and is irrelevant to the drop. Forcing it would only matter for genuinely-NULL frames and would crash (mov edx,[edi] with edi=NULL). DO NOT APPLY.

live_probe_spec_function_boundary_only.constraint: Function-ENTRY hooks only (mid-function near-branch hooks crash, confirmed). Capture: stop -> start(login) -> probe_extractor_live.py --passive ~18s (login burst auto-fetches ~10s in).
live_probe_spec_function_boundary_only.probe_A_handler_slot: Hook FUN_00404610 @0x404610 ENTRY. Read args: param_1=ecx (msgobj), param_3=[esp+8] (hdr). At entry, before the lookup, log: opcode = word[param_3] decoded, parsesys=[param_1+4]. Then on RETURN (hook FUN_00404610 exit OR the caller FUN_00404210 @0x404266 ret site), read *(param_1+0xc) = the resolved handler pointer, and log handler[0] (its vtbl[0]) for opcode 0x2004 vs 0x2006. EXPECT: if handler ptr is 0 for 0x2006 -> handler-not-found (al should be 0, contradicting live -> re-check). If handler ptr non-0 but its vtbl[0] differs from 0x2004's handler vtbl[0] -> THAT is the divergence; RE that 0x2006 handler class's vtbl[0] to see why it does not reach FUN_004ae0d0.
live_probe_spec_function_boundary_only.probe_B_enqueue_call: Hook FUN_004ae0d0 @0x4ae0d0 ENTRY. Log [esp+4]=opcode for every call. EXPECT: 0x2004 appears, 0x2006 does NOT -> confirms the drop is upstream of FUN_004ae0d0 (i.e. inside the 0x2006 handler's vtbl[0], before it would call FUN_004ae0d0). If 0x2006 DOES appear here but never reaches the classifier -> the drop is between FUN_004ae0d0 and FUN_004b8850 (re-check the 0x202/0x204 special-cases and [conn+0x18] state).
live_probe_spec_function_boundary_only.probe_C_parse_message_gate: ONLY if probes A/B are inconclusive (i.e. delivery uses FUN_004029f0 not FUN_00404610 inline): hook FUN_004029f0 @0x4029f0 ENTRY, log param_2 (handler ptr [esp+0x48]) and, on the found path, the two gate results -- read byte[(msgobj+0x54)+0x14] (gate1 FUN_004045a0) and [(+0x54)+8]/[+0xc] (gate2 FUN_00404580). A 0 in either for 0x2006 = the silent drop.
live_probe_spec_function_boundary_only.probe_D_dispatcher_guard: Hook FUN_004ba2b0 @0x4ba2b0 ENTRY (already done -> op2006=0). If a future probe shows 0x2006 DOES reach here, also read byte[base+0x3579cd] at entry: if 1, the top one-shot guard is swallowing 0x2006 (then RE who sets 0x3579cd=1 relative to the 0x2005/0x2006 exchange).

patch_design_pending_probe: No same-length byte patch is justified yet. Once probe A/B pins the divergence, the likely fix shapes are: (a) if the 0x2006 handler-slot is genuinely empty/aliased (registry index collision idx6 vs idx4), patch the REGISTRATION constant or the lookup index math (e.g. FUN_00446ab0/FUN_00446a50 `(param_3+0xe000)` or the per-opcode getter) -- same-length only if an immediate is involved. (b) if a shared handler gates 0x2006 by opcode, NOP the opcode comparison/branch in that handler's vtbl[0] -- same-length 2B `jcc`->`9090` or invert `jcc` opcode. (c) if FUN_004029f0's gate is the drop, the fix is to ensure the body is read on the al=1 path (NOT a 1-line patch -- structural; prefer (a)/(b)). ALL candidates require capstone-verified originalHex and a same-length, rel32-preserving edit; none are pre-authored here because the divergence VA is not yet pinned.
build_integration: tools/logh7_build_playable_client.py --patches <names...> applies tools/client_patches/<name>.json patches[] with drift-check. recvcap is INTENTIONALLY NOT in DEFAULT_STACK (['menufix','dlgfix']). The patches[] array below is EMPTY pending the probe (was C1/C2 -- removed because proven no-op). Do NOT add patches until probe-pinned.
